ucs.title: [LLM] BITTER (T-APT-17) Equation Editor exploit chain — EQNEDT32.EXE spawns shell/downloader (CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0798 weaponized RTF)
ucs.description: Hunts BITTER's signature initial-access chain against South-Asian government/energy targets: a weaponized RTF (delivered via spearphish to Pakistan/Bangladesh/China defense and engineering verticals) abuses the unpatched Microsoft Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) to execute shellcode that drops ArtraDownloader / ZxxZ / MuuyDownloader / WmRAT. EQNEDT32 is OOP-invoked by Office and has no legitimate reason to spawn a child — *any* child is a high-fidelity hit on this group's tradecraft (Forcepoint 2016, Talos 2022 Bangladesh campaign, Proofpoint 2024 WmRAT/MiyaRAT cluster).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE" by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as Computer | where NOT match(user, "\$$") | eval risk_score=90, threat_actor="BITTER (G1002 / T-APT-17)", cve="CVE-2017-11882, CVE-2018-0798" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// BITTER (G1002) — Equation Editor exploitation chain
// EQNEDT32.EXE has no legitimate reason to spawn child processes
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","forfiles.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend SuspiciousChild = FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA  = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          Child      = FileName,
          ChildPath  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA   = SHA256,
          SuspiciousChild,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BITTER (G1002) — Equation Editor exploitation chain (Sentinel SecurityEvent EID 4688)
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "EQNEDT32.EXE"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend SuspiciousChild = ChildBin in~ (_suspicious_children)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, SuspiciousChild
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BITTER APT - Microsoft Equation Editor Spawns Child Process
id: 9c4ad2e1-6f3b-4a7d-8e5f-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects EQNEDT32.EXE spawning any child process. Microsoft Equation Editor was retired
  in 2018 and is exclusively reached via embedded OLE in malicious RTF/DOC files. BITTER
  (G1002 / T-APT-17) operationalises CVE-2017-11882 and CVE-2018-0798 against Pakistan,
  Bangladesh, and Chinese government/energy targets to drop ArtraDownloader, ZxxZ,
  MuuyDownloader, and WmRAT.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1002/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/2022/05/bitter-apt-adds-bangladesh-to-their.html
  - https://www.forcepoint.com/blog/x-labs/bitter-targeted-attack-against-pakistan
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g1002
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Equation Editor support ended in January 2018; legitimate launches in modern enterprises are essentially zero.
level: high
ucs.rationale: BITTER has consistently weaponized Equation Editor RCEs (CVE-2017-11882 confirmed in the Talos 2022 Bangladesh military report; CVE-2018-0798 used in 2020-2024 RTF lures). EQNEDT32.EXE is an out-of-process COM server with no scripting/automation customers — a child process is essentially pathognomonic of exploitation. Other South-Asian-aligned actors (SideWinder, Patchwork, Donot) also touch these

ucs.title: [LLM] BITTER scheduled-task downloader persistence — schtasks /create invoking LOLBin to fetch from DDNS C2
ucs.description: Hunts BITTER's distinctive post-exploit persistence pattern (Talos, Proofpoint, Cisco Secureworks): schtasks.exe is used to register a recurring task that fires curl/PowerShell/mshta/certutil against a Dynamic DNS host (No-IP, ChangeIP, DuckDNS) to pull stage-2 (BitterRAT, Almond RAT, WmRAT, MiyaRAT). The combination of schtasks.exe + an inline LOLBin URL fetch + a DDNS provider in the same command line is rarely benign and matches BITTER's kill chain documented across G1002 reporting.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" Processes.process="*/create*" (Processes.process="*powershell*" OR Processes.process="*cmd /c*" OR Processes.process="*mshta*" OR Processes.process="*curl*" OR Processes.process="*bitsadmin*" OR Processes.process="*certutil*" OR Processes.process="*rundll32*" OR Processes.process="*regsvr32*" OR Processes.process="*wscript*" OR Processes.process="*cscript*" OR Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*ddns.net*" OR Processes.process="*serveftp.com*" OR Processes.process="*linkpc.net*" OR Processes.process="*hopto.org*" OR Processes.process="*sytes.net*" OR Processes.process="*zapto.org*" OR Processes.process="*myftp.org*" OR Processes.process="*duckdns.org*" OR Processes.process="*redirectme.net*" OR Processes.process="*servehttp.com*" OR Processes.process="*ignorelist.com*") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as Computer | where NOT match(user, "\$$") | eval threat_actor="BITTER (G1002 / T-APT-17)" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// BITTER (G1002) — schtasks /create + LOLBin URL/DDNS fetch persistence
let _bitter_ddns = dynamic([
    "ddns.net","serveftp.com","linkpc.net","hopto.org","sytes.net",
    "zapto.org","myftp.org","duckdns.org","redirectme.net","servehttp.com",
    "servepics.com","servegame.com","myvnc.com","ignorelist.com",
    "changeip.com","hopto.me","chickenkiller.com"
]);
let _lolbin_tokens = dynamic([
    "powershell","pwsh","cmd /c","cmd.exe /c","mshta","curl","wget",
    "bitsadmin","certutil","rundll32","regsvr32","wscript","cscript",
    "DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX",
    "http://","https://"
]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create" or ProcessCommandLine has "/CREATE"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend CmdLower = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where CmdLower has_any (_lolbin_tokens) or CmdLower has_any (_bitter_ddns)
| extend HasDDNS = CmdLower has_any (_bitter_ddns)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          TaskCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          HasDDNS,
          IntegrityLvl = ProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BITTER (G1002) — schtasks /create + LOLBin URL/DDNS fetch (Sentinel SecurityEvent EID 4688)
let _bitter_ddns = dynamic([
    "ddns.net","serveftp.com","linkpc.net","hopto.org","sytes.net",
    "zapto.org","myftp.org","duckdns.org","redirectme.net","servehttp.com",
    "changeip.com","chickenkiller.com","ignorelist.com"
]);
let _lolbin_tokens = dynamic([
    "powershell","pwsh","cmd /c","mshta","curl","wget","bitsadmin",
    "certutil","rundll32","regsvr32","wscript","cscript",
    "downloadstring","downloadfile","invoke-webrequest","http://","https://"
]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "schtasks.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend CmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| where CmdLower has "/create"
| where CmdLower has_any (_lolbin_tokens) or CmdLower has_any (_bitter_ddns)
| extend HasDDNS = CmdLower has_any (_bitter_ddns)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          CommandLine, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName,
          HasDDNS, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: BITTER's downloader stages (ArtraDownloader, MuuyDownloader, the WmRAT/MiyaRAT installer chain) all converge on a near-identical persistence primitive: a scheduled task whose action is a one-liner LOLBin reaching out to a No-IP/ChangeIP/DuckDNS host. The actor relies on DDNS rather than acquired domains because of operational tempo (T1568 + T1583.001 in their MITRE list) and abuses native schtasks
